summary: Ukrainian FPV pilots are targeting Russian forces, including attempts to dismount troops from horses before strikes. Russian forces are countering with systems like the "Elka" air defense drone, which intercepted a Ukrainian fixed-wing UAV using kinetic means. The Rubicon Center reported 104 targets neutralized on March 11, 2026, including FPV anti-drone operations against Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs. A Russian infantryman was killed in a bayonet charge against a Ukrainian FPV drone, and a Ukrainian MaxxPro armored vehicle was destroyed by a Russian fiber-optic FPV drone "Barmaley" in Dobropole. Ukrainian forces are deploying increasing numbers of "Molnia" strike and reconnaissance drones, which are difficult to counter due to unknown control frequencies. Ukrainian FPV drones also struck a Russian soldier hiding behind a tree with two simultaneous hits.
